title: Inflamed Gums: A Guide to Causes, Medicinal Herbs, and Remedies
causes_desc: When it comes to inflamed gums, there are several main causes to consider. Poor oral hygiene is a big one - when you don't brush and floss regularly, plaque builds up on your teeth, leading to inflammation and redness in your gums. Gingivitis is another related cause, which is a mild form of gum disease that's often caused by poor oral hygiene. If left untreated, gingivitis can develop into periodontitis, a more serious condition that causes the gums to pull away from the teeth and create pockets where bacteria can multiply. Smoking is also a major contributor to inflamed gums, as the chemicals in tobacco smoke damage the blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the gums, making them more prone to inflammation. Hormonal changes, such as those experienced during pregnancy or menopause, can also cause inflamed gums, as the increased levels of estrogen and progesterone can cause the gums to become more sensitive and reactive.
herbs_desc: Herbs have been used for centuries to help with swollen and inflamed gums. Echinacea purpurea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and fight off infections that cause gum inflammation. It also boosts the immune system to prevent future infections. Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, has antimicrobial properties that can kill bacteria that cause gum disease. Its antioxidant properties also help protect gums from damage caused by free radicals. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a powerful compound called curcumin that reduces inflammation and fights off infections. It also has antiseptic properties that can prevent gum infections from spreading. Melaleuca alternifolia, or tea tree oil, has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and kill bacteria that cause gum disease. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and soothe irritated gums. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent gum infections from spreading. When used together, these herbs can provide a powerful combination of anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and prevent future infections.
preparations_desc: Herbal preparations can be really helpful for inflamed gums. A decoction is a strong liquid made by boiling herbs in water. For inflamed gums, you can try a decoction of calendula or chamomile, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the gums.  A herbal tea can also be useful, as it's easy to drink and can be made with a variety of herbs like peppermint, sage, or echinacea. These herbs have properties that can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum infections.  A tincture is a concentrated liquid made by soaking herbs in a solvent like vodka or glycerin. You can apply a tincture of tea tree oil or eucalyptus to your gums using a cotton swab, which can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria.  A salve is a topical cream or ointment that can be applied directly to the gums. You can make a salve using herbs like aloe vera, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the gums.  You can also take herbal supplements in the form of capsules, which contain dried herbs that have been powdered and filled into a gelatin capsule. For inflamed gums, you can try capsules containing herbs like turmeric or ginger, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain.  Remember to talk to a healthcare professional before trying any new herbal preparations, especially if you're already taking medications or have underlying health conditions.
benefits_desc: Using certain herbs can be really beneficial for inflamed gums. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain. They can also kill the bacteria that cause gum infections, promoting healthy healing. Additionally, some of these herbs have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which prevent further infections and promote a balanced oral environment. Using these herbs can also help reduce bad breath, which is often a symptom of inflamed gums. Furthermore, some herbs have antioxidant properties, which can help repair and protect the gums, preventing further damage. By using these herbs, you can help manage the symptoms of inflamed gums and promote overall gum health.
